title: Case Statements
summary: The case...esac construct provides multi-branch pattern matching in Bash, similar to switch/case in other languages. It is cleaner than long if/elif chains when testing a single variable against multiple patterns.
keyPoints: Multiple patterns per branch: a|b) commands ;;
;; terminates a branch — like break in C switch

exercises.prompt: What keyword closes a case block in Bash?
exercises.explanation: esac is 'case' reversed — a Bash convention also used in other POSIX shells.

exercises.prompt: Complete the case branch that matches either 'yes' or 'y' and echoes 'Confirmed'.
exercises.explanation: The | character separates alternative patterns within a single branch.

exercises.prompt: Write a case statement that takes $1 (start/stop/restart) and echoes the corresponding action, with a default branch that echoes 'Unknown command'.
exercises.hint: Use *) as the catch-all last branch.
